title: Fix VPI force of bit-selected signals
body:
SV tests/70regs/30bkdr/01hdl fails due to a VPI error trying to read forcecable bit-selected signal `dut.q[1]` giving the error 

```
%Error: /svaha/wsnyder/SandBox/homecvs/v4/verilator/include/verilated_vpi.cpp:1356: getForceControlSignals: VPI force or release requested for 'dut.q[1]', but\
 vpiHandle '(nil)' of enable signal 'dut.q[1]__VforceEn' could not be cast to VerilatedVpioVar*. Ensure signal is marked as forceable
```

I improved the t_uvm_dpi test to show this (unchecked on other sims, so might not be perfect), and also added a new test based on the UVM test.

@Christian-Hecken mind taking a look when get a chance?  I think half of this overlaps what you were looking at to fix #7092.

[t_uvm_dpi.v.txt](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/25815978/t_uvm_dpi.v.txt)
[t_uvm_dpi_bkdr.v.txt](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/25815979/t_uvm_dpi_bkdr.v.txt)
[t_uvm_dpi_bkdr.py.txt](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/25815980/t_uvm_dpi_bkdr.py.txt)
code_blocks:
%Error: /svaha/wsnyder/SandBox/homecvs/v4/verilator/include/verilated_vpi.cpp:1356: getForceControlSignals: VPI force or release requested for 'dut.q[1]', but\
 vpiHandle '(nil)' of enable signal 'dut.q[1]__VforceEn' could not be cast to VerilatedVpioVar*. Ensure signal is marked as forceable
